title: Pool Tile Cleaning in Las Vegas- 6 Tips to Clean Your Pool Tiles
author: Clean Living Pool and Spa
excerpt: Learn six effective tips for cleaning your swimming pool tiles, from gathering equipment to using pumice stones. The guide covers removing mineral deposits, calcium scaling, and stubborn grime to keep your pool looking pristine.
body:
6 Tips for Effectively Cleaning Your Pool Tiles

Do you own a swimming pool in your backyard, and you always want to make it look modern and stylish? The tiles in the swimming pool play a massive role in the pool's appearance.

If appropriately maintained, the swimming pool tiles will last for years. You may be removing debris from the pool daily, but it will be stained if you do not clean the tiles.

Aside from stains, grime will also accumulate on the pool tiles. These include mineral deposits, sunscreen, and body oil.

When the water evaporates, the mineral deposits will cling to the pool tiles, which will be hard to remove.

If you are new to pool care, do not worry because we will take you through tips on cleaning your pool tiles. However, how can you identify scaling on tiles before we dive into that?

There are two major types of scaling that you will notice on your pool tiles. They include:

  • Calcium Silicate Deposits - This dark gray type does not flake and will not react to muriatic acid. It is harder to remove once it has stuck on the pool tiles.
  • Calcium Carbonate Deposits - It is usually white and flaky, and it bubbles up when treated with muriatic acid. This is the most difficult type of scale to remove from pool tiles.

These calcium deposits are caused by a pH buildup in the pool water. Other causes include heat and if the swimming pool water supply is hard water.

Nevertheless, swimming pool staining can be seen in a variety of colors. The only way you can prevent the occurrence of these stains is through regular pool cleaning.

Statistics show that about 75% of swimming pool stains are caused by poor maintenance of the pool.

Now that you know the types of swimming pool tile stains, how do you clean them?

Here Are 6 Tips To Cleaning Your Pool Tile in Las Vegas

  1. Gather your equipment and materials.
  2. Remove all the organic matter.
  3. Drop the waterline.
  4. Use a scrubbing brush.
  5. Remove the dirt and grime.
  6. Use a pumice stone.

Tip No. 1: Gather Your Tile Cleaning Equipment and Materials

Before cleaning your swimming pool tiles, ensure you have your equipment and materials ready. The equipment and materials you need are:

Equipment

  • Rubber gloves
  • Pumice stone
  • Plastic bucket
  • Soft-bristled brush
  • Eye protection
  • Skimmer net
  • Pool vacuum
  • Stiff-bristled brush

Materials

  • Commercial tile cleaner
  • Melamine sponge
  • Dishwashing liquid
  • Baking soda
  • Cleaning vinegar
  • Distilled white vinegar

Once you have gathered all the equipment and materials needed to clean your swimming pool tiles, ensure you start with the gentle tile cleaning methods.

This is because you may realize that the more straightforward methods will clean the grime without advancing to thorough methods of cleaning the pool tiles.

Tip No. 2: Remove All The Organic Matter

When you start cleaning your swimming pool tiles, ensure you start by removing all the organic matter, including dirt and debris.

Use a net skimmer and a pool vacuum to remove all the leaves, plastic, grass clipping, and organic matter until the swimming pool water is clean.

Tip No. 3: Drop The Water Line

Drop the waterline slightly below the usual level after removing all the debris from the pool water.

This ensures that you will have an easier time cleaning the swimming pool tiles. You can either clean the pool tiles while you are in the water or from the pool deck.

Tip No. 4: Use a Scrubbing Brush

After dropping the waterline, begin cleaning the pool tiles by scrubbing the mineral deposits using a stiff-bristled brush.

Ensure that you scrub the pool tiles in a circular motion about three feet wide.

If your swimming pool tiles are made of glass, use a soft-bristled brush to prevent any risk of scratching the glass.

Use an old toothbrush for tight and challenging areas to reach using the big brushes.

Tip No. 5: Remove Dirt and Grime

After removing the mineral deposits using the scrubbing brushes, remove the dirt and grime by scrubbing the area using any of these solutions:

  • Dry baking soda and a few drops of water to create a paste.
  • One part water and one part distilled white vinegar.
  • A quarter cup of dishwashing liquid and a gallon of water.
  • Two parts water and one part cleaning vinegar.

Scrub the pool tiles by dipping the scrubbing brush into these solutions, then sour the pool tiles at the waterline.

If the stains become tough to remove, use a melamine sponge to scrub them off.

After scrubbing, splash the pool water over the area for a clean finish. Repeat the same process as you scrub around the swimming pool perimeter.

Tip No. 6: Use a Pumice Stone

If the scrubbing brushes and the cleaning solutions do not remove the grime or mineral deposits, use a pumice stone.

You can purchase a pumice stone from home improvement or swimming pool supplies stores.

The pumice stone will work well because it is made from ground volcanic rock to break the deposits apart.

Ensure that you keep the stone and the pool tiles wet and work slowly in circular motions to prevent excessive scratching.
